uplink.description: Status report: pH 7.20, water temp 25.00 C, turbidity 5.0 NTU, residual chlorine 0.75 mg/L, high-pH + shock/tamper alarm
uplink._note: Expected derived via the ORACLE method: reference/upstream-codec.js decodes this frame to PH 7.2pH, TemperaturewithPH 25C, NTU 5ntu, TemperaturewithNTU -2C (two's complement 0xFF38), Residual 0.75mg/L, HighPHAlarm 1, ShockTamperAlarm Alarm. Synthetic input; bytes[1..2]=0x010E (270 == R900PD01O1).

uplink.description: Status report: pH 6.85, water temp -1.50 C, turbidity 123.4 NTU, residual chlorine 2.50 mg/L, low-turbidity alarm, no tamper
uplink._note: ORACLE method: upstream decodes PH 6.85pH, TemperaturewithPH -1.5C (0xFF6A two's complement), NTU 123.4ntu (0x04D2 /10), Residual 2.5mg/L, LowNTUAlarm 1. Exercises negative temperature and multi-digit turbidity.

uplink.description: Dry-contact output config readback: NormallyHighLevel, 30 s pulse, bound to low-pH + low-turbidity alarms, Channel2
uplink._note: ORACLE method: reference decodes cmd 0x86 (ReadConfigDryContactPointOutRsp) to DryContactPointOutType NormallyHighLevel, OutPluseTime 30s, LowPHAlarm 1 + LowNTUAlarm 1. We diverge intentionally on Channel: upstream has a copy-paste bug (reads bytes[8] instead of bytes[7] for the channel, so it omits it); our codec reads bytes[7]=0x01 -> Channel2, the datasheet-correct field. dry-contact output state -> extra dryContactOut.

uplink.description: Device-info / startup frame (fPort 22, reportType 0) carries no measurement
uplink._note: ORACLE method: upstream returns sw/hw version + datecode (no measurement). reportType byte[3]=0 -> error per output contract (never emit a measurement-less data object).

uplink.description: Config acknowledgement (ConfigReportRsp) on fPort 23 carries no measurement
uplink._note: ORACLE method: upstream returns Cmd ConfigReportRsp / Status Success (an ack, no measurement). Only cmd 0x86 readback is surfaced; everything else on fPort 23 -> error.
ORACLE method: upstream returns errors ['unknown FPort'] for unknown ports. Our message names the supported ports.
